name: RandoMatic

consumes.minimum: 1 active composition + 1 selected property with at least 1 selected keyframe
consumes.description: Reads app.project.activeItem (must be a CompItem), then comp.selectedProperties to detect which properties have selected keyframes and to determine maxDimensionality (1D scalar vs 2D/3D array value). Iterates property.selectedKeys and calls property.keySelected(j) for each key index.

controls.name: Start Range X
controls.role: sets the lower bound of the randomisation range for the X (or scalar) dimension

controls.name: End Range X
controls.role: sets the upper bound of the randomisation range for the X (or scalar) dimension

controls.name: Start Range Y
controls.role: sets the lower bound of the randomisation range for the Y dimension (shown for 2D/3D properties only)

controls.name: End Range Y
controls.role: sets the upper bound of the randomisation range for the Y dimension (shown for 2D/3D properties only)

controls.name: Start Range Z
controls.role: sets the lower bound of the randomisation range for the Z dimension (shown for 3D properties only)

controls.name: End Range Z
controls.role: sets the upper bound of the randomisation range for the Z dimension (shown for 3D properties only)

controls.name: OK
controls.role: validates inputs and applies randomisation to all selected keyframes

controls.name: Cancel
controls.role: closes the dialog without making any changes

functions: validates that an active composition is selected before opening the dialog
validates that at least one property with keyframes is selected before opening the dialog
detects the maximum dimensionality of selected properties to show 1–3 range-input rows (X / Y / Z)
validates all range inputs for non-numeric values using parseFloat and NaN detection
computes a uniform-random value for each dimension using startRange + Math.random() * (endRange − startRange)
applies the random value to every selected keyframe on every selected property via property.setValueAtKey
wraps all keyframe mutations in a single undo group labelled 'RandoMatic'

scriptSim.scenario: success – randomise 2D Position keyframes in range
scriptSim.inputs: Active comp 'Rando Comp' (1920×1080, 24fps, 5s); one Shape layer with a 2D TwoD Position property ('Shape Layer 1/Transform/Position') carrying 3 keyframes at t=0, 1.5, 3 s — all three selected. Dialog range inputs: Start Range X=−50, End Range X=50, Start Range Y=−30, End Range Y=30. OK clicked.

notes: The number of range-input rows is dynamic: 1 row for scalar properties, 2 rows for 2D (e.g. Position), 3 rows for 3D (e.g. Position on a 3D layer). Controls Y and Z only appear when maxDimensionality >= 2 / 3.
Randomisation uses a uniform distribution (Math.random()). Actual keyframe values are non-deterministic; the harness can only assert operation kind and target, not exact values.
The script preserves keyframe timing and interpolation — only the value at each keyframe index is changed via setValueAtKey.
Input validation alerts inline and keeps the dialog open; it does not close on a bad-input OK click.
No AE expressions are written by this script; expressions[] is empty.
